steps.title: Find what “tip” must mean in context
steps.content: The passage says Gogol must <u>tip</u> his head back in order to see the kite high in the sky. This is a physical action — Gogol is tilting or inclining his head backward so he can look upward. The replacement word must describe that exact motion.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Lean: Gogol must “lean his head back” — lean means to tilt or incline, which matches the physical action of angling the head backward to look up. ✓
  • Choice B — Create: Gogol must “create his head back” — create means to make or produce something; it cannot describe a physical head movement. ✗
  • Choice C — Pay: Gogol must “pay his head back” — pay means to give money or settle a debt, entirely unrelated to a body position. ✗
  • Choice D — Teach: Gogol must “teach his head back” — teach means to instruct, which makes no sense as a physical action. ✗
That is the correct answer.
